codeReview.summary: The scaffold creates the requested monorepo layout and validation setup, but the shared classifyMethod() helper — one of the feature's core deliverables — encodes an incorrect Codex classification rule for non-tool item lifecycle messages.

issues.description: classifyMethod() returns `agentMessage` for `item/started` / `item/completed` whenever the item is not a tool call. The mission's Codex classification contract only treats `item/agentMessage/delta` as `agentMessage`; non-tool lifecycle items should stay non-agent/unknown instead of being surfaced as chat output. Because this helper is the shared protocol primitive promised by the scaffold, TypeScript consumers built on it will misclassify lifecycle traffic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Repository docs are not aligned on protocol terminology and state modeling: architecture.md still describes shared message types as `approval` and shared session enums as `online/offline/evicted`, while the scaffolded shared package introduces `serverRequest` and `idle/executing/waitingApproval`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:52,96-98,107 use `approval` and `online/offline/evicted`; shared/src/types.ts:6-15 and shared/src/protocol.ts:72-73 define `waitingApproval` and `serverRequest`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ts-worker skill assumes every TypeScript task should start with a RED test, but scaffold features like this one explicitly allow zero tests. The worker followed the scaffold contract while still marking `followedProcedure: true`, so the skill likely needs a documented exception for setup-only tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ts-worker/SKILL.md:26-32 require failing tests first; /home/dl/.factory/missions/33afaa22-b81b-48d3-b9b3-131d99d630c7/handoffs/2026-03-31T05-17-13-206Z__project-scaffold__84e6471d-44f0-4cda-92b6-52bda1a35357.json:62-70 records `tests.added: []` and `followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 58ea3a3401e79aa0b01d6468b0b94fc0e77c5b97. The scaffold mostly matches the requested repository layout, but the shared protocol classifier — a core scaffold deliverable — misclassifies non-tool `item/started` / `item/completed` messages as `agentMessage`, which is a blocking contract issue. I also noted doc/skill drift around protocol terminology and zero-test scaffold work.
